# Agent Assignments: Feature 039 — Standalone /infographic Command

**Feature**: 039-standalone-infographic-command
**Generated**: 2026-03-28
**Generated by**: team-lead
**Source**: `specs/039-standalone-infographic-command/tasks.md`

---

## Feasibility Summary

| Dimension | Assessment |
|-----------|------------|
| Effort | 34 tasks across 6 phases; all deliverables are markdown/YAML files |
| Capacity | 2 agents required (senior-backend-engineer, tester); code-reviewer for final gate |
| Timeline | MVP (9 tasks) in 1 session; full delivery (34 tasks) in 1-2 sessions |
| Dependencies | Phase 1 blocks Phase 2; Phase 1+3 block Phase 4; Phase 1+2 block Phase 5 |
| Confidence | 85% |

**Verdict**: FEASIBLE

---

## Agent Registry (Valid Names)

All assignments use exact agent names from `.claude/agents/_README.md`:

| Agent | Registry Role | Assignment Rationale |
|-------|---------------|----------------------|
| `senior-backend-engineer` | Backend Engineer | Agent/command file authoring, adapter updates, documentation |
| `tester` | QA Engineer | Validation, acceptance testing, end-to-end workflow verification |
| `code-reviewer` | Code Quality Analyst | Cross-wave quality gate reviews |

## Parallel Execution Waves

### Wave 1: Foundation + Pipeline Cleanup (Parallel)

**Prerequisite**: None — start immediately
**Estimated Duration**: 25-35 minutes

```
Track A: Phase 1 — Agent Enhancement (T001-T004)
  Agent: senior-backend-engineer
  File:  .claude/agents/tachi/threat-infographic.md
  Tasks: T001 → T002 → T003 → T004 (sequential, same file)

Track B: Phase 3 — Pipeline Cleanup, threat-model.md (T010-T014)
  Agent: senior-backend-engineer
  File:  .claude/commands/threat-model.md
  Tasks: T010 [P] → T011 [P] → T012 → T013 → T014

Track C: Phase 3 — Pipeline Cleanup, orchestrator.md (T015-T016)
  Agent: senior-backend-engineer
  File:  .claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md
  Tasks: T015 → T016
```

**Parallelism**: 3 tracks (different files, no cross-dependencies)

**Quality Gate QG-1**: Verify before proceeding to Wave 2:
- [ ] Infographic agent has dual-path data extraction (T001-T004)
- [ ] `threat-model.md` has no Phase 6 references (T010-T014)
- [ ] `orchestrator.md` has no Phase 6 dispatch (T015-T016)
- [ ] Reviewer: `code-reviewer`

---

### Wave 2: Command Creation (Sequential)

**Prerequisite**: Wave 1 Track A complete (Phase 1 — agent must support dual-path)
**Estimated Duration**: 20-30 minutes

```
Track A: Phase 2 — /infographic Command (T005-T009)
  Agent: senior-backend-engineer
  File:  .claude/commands/infographic.md
  Tasks: T005 → T006 → T007 → T008 → T009 (sequential, builds command incrementally)
```

**Parallelism**: 1 track (single file, sequential build)

**Quality Gate QG-2 (MVP Checkpoint)**: Verify before proceeding to Wave 3:
- [ ] `/infographic` command exists with all 4 steps
- [ ] Auto-detection works for both threats.md and risk-scores.md
- [ ] Template selection flag parses correctly
- [ ] Quality checklist present in command
- [ ] Reviewer: `code-reviewer`

---

### Wave 3: Adapter Sync (Maximum Parallel)

**Prerequisite**: Wave 1 complete (Phase 1 + Phase 3 canonical files finalized)
**Estimated Duration**: 20-30 minutes

```
Track A: Orchestrator Adapters (T017-T021)
  Agent: senior-backend-engineer
  Tasks: T017 [P], T018 [P], T019 [P], T020 [P], T021 [P]
  Note:  All parallel — each targets a different adapter platform file

Track B: Command Adapters (T022-T023)
  Agent: senior-backend-engineer
  Tasks: T022 [P], T023 [P]
  Note:  T022 mirrors threat-model cleanup; T023 copies /infographic command

Track C: Infographic Agent Adapters (T024-T027)
  Agent: senior-backend-engineer
  Tasks: T024 [P], T025 [P], T026 [P], T027 [P]
  Note:  All parallel — each mirrors T001-T004 to a different adapter platform
```

**Parallelism**: Up to 11 tasks across 11 files (maximum parallel opportunity)

**Quality Gate QG-3**: Verify before proceeding to Wave 4:
- [ ] All 5 orchestrator adapters reference 5 phases (not 6)
- [ ] claude-code threat-model adapter has no Phase 6 (T022)
- [ ] claude-code infographic command adapter exists (T023)
- [ ] All 4 infographic agent adapters have dual-path extraction (T024-T027)
- [ ] Reviewer: `code-reviewer`

---

### Wave 4: Validation + Polish (Parallel)

**Prerequisite**: All previous waves complete
**Estimated Duration**: 15-25 minutes

```
Track A: Phase 5 — Regeneration Validation (T028-T030)
  Agent: tester
  Tasks: T028 → T029 → T030 (sequential — each validates a different aspect)

Track B: Phase 6 — Documentation Polish (T031-T032)
  Agent: senior-backend-engineer
  Tasks: T031 [P], T032 [P]
  Note:  Parallel — different files

Track C: Phase 6 — End-to-End Validation (T033-T034)
  Agent: tester
  Tasks: T033, T034
  Note:  After Track A completes (T033 depends on command working)
```

**Parallelism**: 2-3 tracks (validation and docs update simultaneously)

**Quality Gate QG-4 (Final)**: Verify before feature sign-off:
- [ ] Auto-detection selects risk-scores.md over threats.md (T028)
- [ ] Output uses composite scores, not qualitative severity (T029)
- [ ] Idempotent overwrite works cleanly (T030)
- [ ] SKILL.md and README updated (T031-T032)
- [ ] End-to-end workflow: `/threat-model` then `/risk-score` then `/infographic` succeeds (T033)
- [ ] Single-template generation works for both templates (T034)
- [ ] Reviewer: `code-reviewer`

---

## Workload Distribution

| Agent | Tasks Assigned | Waves Active | Load |
|-------|---------------|--------------|------|
| `senior-backend-engineer` | 27 (T001-T027, T031-T032) | Wave 1, 2, 3, 4 | Primary executor |
| `tester` | 5 (T028-T030, T033-T034) | Wave 4 | Validation only |
| `code-reviewer` | 4 quality gates (QG-1 through QG-4) | Between waves | Gate reviews |

**Load Assessment**: Balanced for a knowledge-system project where all deliverables are markdown files. `senior-backend-engineer` handles all file authoring. `tester` handles all validation. `code-reviewer` ensures quality between waves. No agent exceeds 80% capacity within any single wave due to parallelism.

---

| Wave | Phase(s) | Tasks | Tracks | Est. Duration | Cumulative |
|------|----------|-------|--------|---------------|------------|
| Wave 1 | Phase 1 + Phase 3 | 11 | 3 parallel | 25-35 min | 25-35 min |
| QG-1 | Gate review | -- | 1 | 5-10 min | 30-45 min |
| Wave 2 | Phase 2 | 5 | 1 sequential | 20-30 min | 50-75 min |
| QG-2 | Gate review (MVP) | -- | 1 | 5-10 min | 55-85 min |
| Wave 3 | Phase 4 | 11 | 11 parallel | 20-30 min | 75-115 min |
| QG-3 | Gate review | -- | 1 | 5-10 min | 80-125 min |
| Wave 4 | Phase 5 + Phase 6 | 7 | 3 parallel | 15-25 min | 95-150 min |
| QG-4 | Final gate review | -- | 1 | 5-10 min | 100-160 min |

**Optimistic**: ~100 minutes (1.7 hours)
**Realistic**: ~130 minutes (2.2 hours)
**Pessimistic**: ~160 minutes (2.7 hours)

---

## MVP vs Full Delivery

### MVP (Phase 1 + Phase 2): 9 tasks

- Wave 1 Track A (T001-T004) then Wave 2 (T005-T009)
- Delivers: Working `/infographic` command with dual-path detection
- Users can run `/infographic` immediately after MVP
- Estimated: 50-75 minutes

### Full Delivery (All Phases): 34 tasks

- All 4 waves with quality gates
- Delivers: Clean 5-phase pipeline across all adapter platforms
- Estimated: 100-160 minutes

---

## Notes

- All tasks produce markdown files only (knowledge-system project)
- T011 may be a no-op per architect review (skip-infographic refs may live in orchestrator, not threat-model.md) — `senior-backend-engineer` should verify before executing
- Adapter tasks (T017-T027) mirror canonical changes — high automation potential
- Wave 3 offers maximum parallelism (11 independent files)
- No `frontend-developer`, `security-analyst`, or `devops` agents needed for this feature

---

**End of Agent Assignments**
